concerns.text: The plan targets Nside=2048 maps on a 7 GB RAM limit. A single Nside=2048 HEALPix map (float64) requires ~12 MB, but the full covariance matrix for likelihood evaluation or the storage of 1000 simulations at this resolution (12 MB * 1000 = 12 GB) exceeds the 7 GB constraint. The plan mentions 'sub-sampling' but does not define the statistical validity of subsampling multipoles for BipoSH/LIV constraints, which may destroy the signal-to-noise ratio required to detect the specific SME coefficient.

The plan proposes a likelihood-ratio test and MCMC to constrain the SME coefficient k_(V)00^(5) using a 'forward-model simulation' (FR-008). However, the research.md does not specify the mathematical mapping between the SME coefficient and the BipoSH coefficients or the power spectrum asymmetry. Without an explicit forward model (e.g., how k_(V)00^(5) modifies C_l or A_LM), the MCMC likelihood function cannot be constructed, rendering the inference step methodologically impossible as described.

The plan states: 'Generate a null distribution using 1,000 isotropic Gaussian simulations... to cross-validate any detected anisotropy.' It fails to specify how these simulations incorporate the specific instrumental noise and beam asymmetries of the Planck SMICA component separation. If the null simulations are pure Gaussian CMB without realistic noise/beam systematics, any detected anisotropy in the real data (which includes these systematics) will be falsely attributed to LIV, violating the 'Statistical Anisotropy Validation' principle.

The plan proposes using the Hanson & Lewis estimator for dipole modulation to test for Lorentz violation. While dipole modulation is a signature of anisotropy, the plan does not distinguish between a simple dipole modulation (which could be a foreground or systematic) and the specific quadrupole/octupole patterns predicted by the photon-sector SME coefficient k_(V)00^(5). Using a dipole estimator to constrain a coefficient that primarily affects higher-order multipoles or specific BipoSH modes lacks construct validity.
